bugs.error_message: unknown type name 'NewRpgStatus'; did you mean 'ai::NewRpgStatus'?
bugs.root_cause: PlayerbotAIConfig.h included NewRpgInfo.h which pulls WorldPosition.h → PathFinder.h → MoveMapSharedDefines.h → Detour/Include/DetourNavMesh.h. The Detour include path is not available in mangosd TU. The enum needed qualifying as ai::NewRpgStatus.
bugs.fix: Removed #include 'src/Ai/World/Rpg/NewRpgInfo.h' from PlayerbotAIConfig.h. Replaced with forward declaration: namespace ai { enum NewRpgStatus : int; }. Added #include + using namespace ai in PlayerbotAIConfig.cpp only.

bugs.error_message: use of undeclared identifier 'botAI' (many instances in NewRpgBaseAction.cpp)
bugs.root_cause: AC code uses variable name 'botAI' for PlayerbotAI* pointer. cmangos action base class uses 'ai'. Bulk copy of AC source brought 'botAI' references throughout.
bugs.fix: replace_all 'botAI' → 'ai'. Side effect: 'PlayerbotAI' became 'Playerai' due to substring collision. Fixed with second replace_all 'Playerai' → 'PlayerbotAI'.

bugs.error_message: member function 'IsInFlight' not found in 'Player' / no member named 'Dismount'
bugs.root_cause: AC Player API uses IsInFlight() and Dismount(). cmangos Player has IsTaxiFlying() and Unmount() respectively.
Attempted to include WorldTimer.h directly. It is available via playerbot.h chain — no direct include needed.
bugs.fix: Removed #include 'Server/WorldTimer.h'. WorldTimer::getMSTime() / getMSTimeDiff() work without explicit include.

bugs.error_message: GO-too-far log prints garbage count like 654864228 times
bugs.root_cause: uint32& failCount referenced a std::map entry that was erased on the same call before the log read the reference — dangling reference, UB garbage value.
bugs.fix: Changed 'uint32& failCount = ++ai->questGoFailCount[id]' to 'uint32 failCount = ++ai->questGoFailCount[id]'. Value copy before erase — log reads safe copy.

bugs.error_message: CHANGE_ZONE short-dist branch fires at up to 479 yards — bots en-route trigger it, waypoints never built, loop every 3s
bugs.root_cause: Waypoint build condition dist>500.0f too wide — bots within 150-500 yards skip waypoint building, fall into direct MoveFarTo loop with waypointsBuilt=0
bugs.fix: Changed threshold 500.0f to 150.0f — bots at 150-500 yards now get TravelNode route built

bugs.error_message: Yamelia/Kaadze repeatedly re-select same bad transport dock node after BOAT-3 guard rejects it (9+ hits per bot per run)
bugs.root_cause: No per-bot memory of rejected transport legs — waypointsBuilt resets on prune, same bad destDock re-selected on every route rebuild
bugs.fix: Added rejectedTransportDocks vector to ChangeZone struct; BOAT-3 guard pushes rejected destDock into cache; route builder skips any destDock within 1 yard of cached rejection

bugs.error_message: Critical Error: A condition which must never be false was found to be false. Server was shut down to protect data integrity. GetMap(): m_currMap
bugs.root_cause: Bot AI tick calls bot->GetMap() (asserting) while bot is being teleported or removed from world — m_currMap is null during this window. Preceded by AbstractPathMovementGenerator::Initialize Path empty + spline-enabled-but-not-moving warnings indicating broken movement state across map transitions.
bugs.fix: IMPLEMENTED. 1) Added IsInWorld()/IsBeingTeleported() guard at top of PlayerbotAI::UpdateAI(). 2) Guarded all bot->GetMap() calls in NewRpgBaseAction.cpp with IsInWorld()/IsBeingTeleported() checks. 3) Guarded bot->GetMap() calls in MovementActions.cpp (PlayerRelocation, IsInLineOfSight, GetReachableRandomPointOnGround). 4) Added GetMotionMaster()->Clear() before HandleMoveWorldportAckOpcode() in HandleTeleportAck() to clear stale spline state on far teleport.
